batch_objective: US-007: placeholder operation classes for special operations on loaded HEX/S19 files (e.g. CRC, extract, split per memory segment) — wired into the code now so each can later be filled with real requirements without re-plumbing. Operator's working contract: input = the loaded HEX/S19 file, output = identical to input (identity passthrough); explicitly open to a better pattern proposed at Phase 1. One placeholder will receive real requirements in a follow-up batch/iteration. Scope = this story ONLY (operator decision at init): US-006 hex-compare stays queued; batch-07 A-7 candidates (manifest writer, HEX emitter, E2E pilot, knee test) remain queued.
batch_layout: Each batch archived under .dev-flow/<YYYY-MM-DD>-batch-NN/. Living docs (REQUIREMENTS.md, tests/, docs/diagrams/) kept current. On batch advance, the closing batch's state.json is snapshotted into its folder as state-snapshot-at-close.json before the shared state.json is trimmed to the new batch.

At the I1 gate the operator established a NEW cross-batch convention (recorded as C-7 in 01-requirements.md §6.2 + persistent memory project_operations_module_conventions.md): each operation's REAL behavior gets its OWN HLR/LLR set, SEPARATE from app requirements, co-located with the module at s19_app/tui/operations/requirements/REQ-<operation_id>.md (created by that operation's fill-in batch); app-level docs (REQUIREMENTS.md, batch 01-requirements) carry REFERENCES only. Operator intent: operations may become ad-hoc functions reused in other applications — requirements travel with the module. Two operator answers at this gate: (1) co-located location CONFIRMED; (2) LoadedFile input-contract decoupling DEFERRED to the first fill-in (R-2 updated): the first real operation's local requirements define the neutral input contract and land the adaptation once. No LLR statement/threshold changed (informative §6.2/§6.3 additions only — no audit row required); I1 code unaffected (package already app-import-free by probe). Operator follow-up: 'Si choca con lo actual, relégalo al siguiente batch' — orchestrator verified NO clash (convention is prospective only; the one potential clash, LoadedFile decoupling, was already deferred). I1 then APPROVED ('Apruebo').
